title: Status, Events, Logs, Metrics, Traces, Audit, and Layered Incident Response · Cluster Case: K8s Metrics Resource System
summary: A release has green desired replicas but user failures, overwritten events, restarted containers, missing cluster logs, high-cardinality metrics, and no cross-layer correlation. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s metrics resource system increment in cluster and workload incident evidence record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish resource metrics, component metrics, application metrics, state metrics, cardinality, scrape, storage, alert, and autoscaling evidence.
Collect current and previous container logs, multi-container streams, timestamps, kubelet and runtime logs, rotation, retention, and node-loss boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Metrics Resource System · Theory K8s Metrics Resource System
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory workload restarts after revision 3; logs rotated, metrics aggregate away one replica, events are delayed, and an operator deletes the Pod first. Preserve object status, events, current and previous logs, metrics, traces, node evidence, and a causal timeline.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the K8s Metrics Resource System contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example workload restarts after revision 3; logs rotated, metrics aggregate away one replica, events are delayed, and an operator deletes the Pod first. Preserve object status, events, current and previous logs, metrics, traces, node evidence, and a causal timeline. Competency probe: Distinguish resource metrics, component metrics, application metrics, state metrics, cardinality, scrape, storage, alert, and autoscaling evidence. A defensible solution applies K8s Metrics Resource System, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to cluster and workload incident evidence record.

content.code:
# Worked focus: k8s-metrics-resource-system — Distinguish resource metrics, component metrics, application metrics, state metrics, cardinality, scrape, storage, alert, and autoscaling evidence.
  # Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-observability-debugging-incidents-5
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-observability-debugging-incidents-5
    artifact: incident-evidence.yaml
    decision: worked k8s observability debugging incidents 5
    environment: managed canary cluster with provider-specific load balancing
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      correlation: release-uid-request-trace
      status: generation-and-conditions
      events: timestamped-short-retention
      logs: current-previous-and-node
      metrics: resource-system-application
      audit: identity-verb-resource
      diagnosis: first-causal-layer
    verify: kubectl get deployment,pod,endpointslice -o json && kubectl logs status-api --previous --timestamps
    failure: Reject green replica count as user proof, restart as diagnosis, events as durable history, kubectl logs as archival storage, or unbounded cardinality.
    repair: Preserve identity and time, correlate desired and observed layers, reproduce one fault cleanly, retain the regression, and document telemetry limits.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.text: metrics-server provides durable application monitoring, dashboards, and alert history. This fails the K8s Metrics Resource System contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

# Evidence: k8s-metrics-resource-system-hvruia
  - competency: k8s-metrics-resource-system
    artifact: incident-evidence.yaml
    decision: metrics resource system
    environment: high-latency edge cluster with intermittent control-plane access
    fault-injection: remove DNS egress from an otherwise ready workload
    retained-proof: EndpointSlice DNS policy route and packet-path correlation
    case-key: hvruia
    change: |
      correlation: release-uid-request-trace

      diagnosis: first-causal-layer
    verify: kubectl get deployment,pod,endpointslice -o json && kubectl logs status-api --previous --timestamps
    failure: Reject green replica count as user proof, restart as diagnosis, events as durable history, kubectl logs as archival storage, or unbounded cardinality.
